title: Content-aware anomaly detection and diagnosis
abstract: Methods and systems for detecting a system fault include determining a network of broken correlations for a current timestamp, relative to a predicted set of correlations, based on a current set of sensor data. The network of broken correlations for the current timestamp is compared to networks of broken correlations for previous timestamps to determine a fault propagation pattern. It is determined whether a fault has occurred based on the fault propagation pattern. A system management action is performed if a fault has occurred.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for detecting a system fault, comprising: determining a network of broken correlations for a current timestamp, relative to a predicted set of correlations, based on a current set of sensor data; comparing the network of broken correlations for the current timestamp to networks of broken correlations for previous timestamps to determine a fault propagation pattern; determining whether a fault has occurred based on the fault propagation pattern using a processor; and performing a system management action if a fault has occurred. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the network of broken correlations comprises determining which predicted correlations from a prediction model are satisfied and which are not. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising training the prediction model based on sensor data for a period of normal system operation. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein comparing the network of broken correlations for the current timestamp to networks of broken correlations for previous timestamps comprises determining a precision curve and a recall curve. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein comparing the network of broken correlations for the current timestamp to networks of broken correlations for previous timestamps further comprises smoothing the precision curve and the recall curve. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein comparing the network of broken correlations for the current timestamp to networks of broken correlations for previous timestamps further comprises determining a time range between the current timestamp and a first timestamp at which a value of the precision curve drops below a threshold. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein comparing the network of broken correlations for the current timestamp to networks of broken correlations for previous timestamps further comprises determining a behavior of the recall curve within the time range. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein determining whether a fault has occurred comprises determining that a fault has occurred if the recall curve increases monotonically in the time range. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein performing a system management action comprises changing a state of the system in response to the detected fault. 10. A system for detecting a fault, comprising: 

 an invariant graph module configured to determine a network of broken correlations for a current timestamp, relative to a predicted set of correlations, based on a current set of sensor data; an invariant comparison module comprising a processor configured to compare the network of broken correlations for the current timestamp to networks of broken correlations for previous timestamps to determine a fault propagation pattern and to determine whether a fault has occurred based on the fault propagation pattern; and a fault management module configured to perform a management action if a fault has occurred. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the invariant graph module is further configured to determine which predicted correlations from a prediction model are satisfied and which are not. 12. The system of claim 11, further comprising a training module configured to train the prediction model based on sensor data for a period of normal system operation. 13. The system of claim 10, wherein the invariant comparison module is further configured to determine a precision curve and a recall curve. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the invariant comparison module is further configured to determine smooth the precision curve and the recall curve. 15. The system of claim 13, wherein the invariant comparison module is further configured to determine a time range between the current timestamp and a first timestamp at which a value of the precision curve drops below a threshold. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the invariant comparison module is further configured to determine a behavior of the recall curve within the time range. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the invariant comparison module is further configured to determine that a fault has occurred if the recall curve increases monotonically in the time range. 18. The system of claim 10, the fault management module is further configured to change a state of the system in response to the detected fault.
